title: CODECtools
text: Denice Ross, U.S. Chief Data Scientist, declared “open data necessary sufficient drive type action need create equitable society.”2 Open data can fall short driving action equitable. Disaggregating data sensitive attributes, like race ethnicity, can elucidate inequities otherwise remain hidden.3 White House’s Equitable Data Working Group4 defined equitable data “allow rigorous assessment extent government programs policies yield consistently fair, just, impartial treatment individuals.” advise equitable data “illuminate opportunities targeted actions result demonstrably improved outcomes underserved communities.” group recommended make disaggregated data norm “… intentional data collected shared, well data protected exacerbate vulnerability members underserved communities, many face heightened risk harm privacy protected.”

title: CODECtools
text: Data people sharing data, privacy spectrum tradeoffs risks benefits individuals populations.5 Data collected individual-level one organization often shared another organization due legal restrictions organization-specific data governance policies. 6 often interested community-level (e.g. neighborhood, census tract, ZIP code) data disaggregated gender, race, sensitive attributes. Achieving data harmonization upstream storage allows contribution disaggregated, community-level data without disclosing individual-level data sharing across organizations. Using specifications, data assembled transparently reproducibly, data structure can automatically validated. saves time resources, leading increased efficiency accelerated innovation. single point data consumption provides ownership process harmonizing data integrates well data governance, importantly, can provide data consumed multiple ways (e.g. dashboard, tabular data file, API). Creating maintaining open community-level data resource equips entire community data-powered decision making boosts organizational trustworthiness. Demonstrating reliability capability appropriately managing shared data helps earn trust organizations communities intended served.7 . . . Figure balance “scientific utility” “security privacy” (pivoting “equitable access”) . . .

title: CODECtools
text: Developed Open Knowledge Foundation, frictionless standards set patterns describing data, including datasets (Data Package), files (Data Resource), tables (Table Schema). Data Package simple container format used describe package collection data metadata, including schemas. metadata contained specific file (separate data file), usually written JSON YAML, describes something specific Frictionless Standard: Table Schema: describes tabular file providing dimension, field data types, relations, constraints Data Resource: describes exact tabular file providing path file details like title, description, others Tabular Data Resource = Data Resource + Table Schema CSV dialect: describes formatting specific various dialects CSV files Data Package & Tabular Data Package: describes collection tabular files providing data resource information along general information package , license, authors, metadata See CODEC tabular-data-resource specifications read CoDEC specifications data, metadata, file structure.

title: CODEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: Data specified average values total counts census tract geographies specific year (year month). required census tract identifer year (year month) columns CODEC tabular-data-resource can used link spatiotemporal health information.

title: CODEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: tabular data must specify geography, CODEC tabular-data-resource MUST include census tract column named census_tract_id_{year}, {year} replaced decennial vintage census tract geographies used create dataset (.e., census_tract_id_2000, census_tract_id_2010, census_tract_id_2020). census tract identifier column MUST contain 11-digit GEOID identifiers census tracts Hamilton County (GEOID: 39061). list required census tract identifiers 2000, 2010, 2020 available {{cincy}} R package (e.g., cincy::tract_tigris_2010). CODEC tdr created census tract level link URL contains code descriptive README file data harmonized (e.g., areal interpolation) census tract geographies.

title: CODEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: Year (month) temporal variables CODEC tdr MUST “tidy” format row represents one time point observations. allows updating datasets newer data without changing field metadata. CODEC tdr MUST include column called year contains integers representing year data collected (e.g., 2018, 2023). also MAY contain month column, case unique combination year month columns represent calendar month data collected (e.g., “2023” “11” together represent November 2023).

title: CODEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: CODEC tabular-data-resource (.e., tdr) consists directory MUST contain exactly one data (.csv) file exactly one metadata file (tabular-data-resource.yaml). name directory name CSV file containing data MUST identical name property. example, files MUST encoded using UTF-8 newlines encoded either \n \r\n. data file MUST follow RFC 4180 standard CSV files. addition: filename MUST end .csv first row MUST header row, containing unique name field value missing, MUST represented either NA empty string (``) metadata file MUST YAML file named tabular-data-resource.yaml adhering metadata specifications.

title: CODEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: Metadata information data, contain data . example, CSV file tell R (software) anything including general information like name, title, description, homepage, well details column, including names, titles, types, formats, constraints. metadata CODEC tabular-data-resource represented hierarchical list specific format. disk, metadata stored separately data tabular-data-resource.yaml file R, stored attributes data.frame. CODEC specifications based Frictionless Data Resource, Table Schema, Tabular Data Resource standards. metadata CODEC tabular-data-resource hierarchically composed different descriptors :  property (“metadata property”) named values used describe data resource. value properties single character string (e.g., name = "my_data"), lists. schema (“table schema”) special property list information fields (columns) tabular-data-resource. schema includes list fields, well value used denote missingness fields primary foreign keys. fields (“field descriptors”) special schema descriptor list fields tabular-data-resource, different descriptors containing field-specific information. CODEC tabular-data-resource MUST contain name path descriptors. properties, schema, fields MAY present, MUST one : fields CSV data file MUST described metadata vice-versa.
code: ## Warning: Returning more (or less) than 1 row per `summarise()` group was deprecated in ## dplyr 1.1.0. ## ℹ Please use `reframe()` instead. ## ℹ When switching from `summarise()` to `reframe()`, remember that `reframe()` ##   always returns an ungrouped data frame and adjust accordingly. ## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was ## generated.

title: CODEC tabular-data-resource specifications
text: example CODEC tabular-data-resource looks like: CSV data file example CODEC tdr contain values vintage 2020 census tracts Hamilton County, first last five shown : CODEC specifications versioned {CODECtools}, article describes version 0.5.0.
code: name: tract_poverty path: tract_poverty.csv title: Fraction of Census Tract Households in Poverty version: 1.2.1 description: measures derived from the 5-year American Community Survey schema:   fields:     census_tract_id_2010:       name: census_tract_id_2010       title: Census Tract       description: 2010 vintage census tract identifier       type: string     year:       name: year       title: Year       type: integer     fraction_poverty:       name: fraction_poverty       title: Fraction of Households in Poverty       type: number year, census_tract_id_2020, fraction_poverty 2020, 39061021508, 0.057 2020, 39061021421, 0.031 2020, 39061023300, 0.030 2020, 39061002000, 0.098 2020, 39061002500, 0.442  ...      ...       ...  2020, 39061021604, 0.259 2020, 39061024700, 0.062 2020, 39061026102, 0.154 2020, 39061023501, 0.046 2020, 39061009800, 0.391

title: Curating metadata for tabular data in R using attributes
text: create simple dataset example:
code: d <-   tibble::tibble(     id = c("A01", "A02", "A03"),     date = as.Date(c("2022-07-25", "2018-07-10", "2013-08-15")),     measure = c(12.8, 13.9, 15.6),     rating = factor(c("good", "best", "best"), levels = c("good", "better", "best")),     ranking = as.integer(c(14, 17, 19)),     impt = c(FALSE, TRUE, TRUE)   )

title: Curating metadata for tabular data in R using attributes
text: creating tabular dataset R, data-specific metadata (.e., “properties”) can stored attributes R object (e.g., data.frame tibble). Note doesn’t change data values. R, object’s attributes stored list. attributes (?attributes) treated specially R (e.g., class, names, row.names, comment) usually shouldn’t modified. Although attributes (including ones added ) available list (?attributes), can use function extract attributes represent metadata descriptors tibble.
code: d <- d |>   add_attrs(     name = "mydata",     title = "My Data",     version = "0.1.0",     homepage = "https://geomarker.io/CODECtools"   ) glimpse_attr(d) |>  knitr::kable()

title: Curating metadata for tabular data in R using attributes
text: Similarly, can add column-specific attributes (.e., “schema”). metadata functions follow tidy design principles, making simple expressively concisely add metadata using pipes:
code: d <-   d |>   add_col_attrs(id, title = "Identifier", description = "unique identifier") |>   add_col_attrs(date, title = "Date", description = "date of observation") |>   add_col_attrs(measure, title = "Measure", description = "measured quantity") |>   add_col_attrs(rating, title = "Rating", description = "ordered ranking of observation") |>   add_col_attrs(ranking, title = "Ranking", description = "rank of the observation") |>   add_col_attrs(impt, title = "Important", description = "true if this observation is important")

what: Adding column-specific metadata properties based on R classes
title: Curating metadata for tabular data in R using attributes
text: Automatically add name, type enum schema column data based class: Like descriptors, helper function retrieve schema tibble: See Reading Writing Tabular Data Resources details save tabular-data-resource disk.

title: Reading and writing tabular data resources
text: Following example, metadata set tibble’s attributes, can save tabular data resource CSV file accompanying tabular-data-resource.yaml: name attribute supplied tibble used name newly created folder CSV file containing data. Metadata extracted supplied tibble’s attributes saved tabular-data-resource.yaml file lives alongside data file newly created directory: Additionally, profile set tabular-data-resource included metadata. allows reading, modifying, writing tabular-data-resources software well. (save just tabular-data-resource.yaml file, use write_tdr().)

what: Reading a tabular-data-resource from disk or the web
title: Reading and writing tabular data resources
text: Since tabular-data-resource.yaml file defines relative location CSV data file path attribute, specifying file (folder contains ) enough read tabular-data-resource disk restore attributes column classes R: tdr_file URL, tabular-data-resource CSV data files automatically downloaded first:
code: mydata <- read_tdr_csv("mydata") mydata #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   id    date       measure rating ranking impt  #>   <chr> <date>       <dbl> <fct>    <int> <lgl> #> 1 A01   2022-07-25    12.8 good        14 FALSE #> 2 A02   2018-07-10    13.9 best        17 TRUE  #> 3 A03   2013-08-15    15.6 best        19 TRUE glimpse_tdr(mydata) #> $attributes #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   name     value                           #>   <chr>    <chr>                           #> 1 profile  tabular-data-resource           #> 2 name     mydata                          #> 3 path     mydata.csv                      #> 4 version  0.1.0                           #> 5 title    My Data                         #> 6 homepage https://geomarker.io/CODECtools #>  #> $schema #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   name    title      description                           type    constraints   #>   <chr>   <chr>      <chr>                                 <chr>   <chr>         #> 1 id      Identifier unique identifier                     string  NA            #> 2 date    Date       date of observation                   date    NA            #> 3 measure Measure    measured quantity                     number  NA            #> 4 rating  Rating     ordered ranking of observation        string  good, better… #> 5 ranking Ranking    rank of the observation               integer NA            #> 6 impt    Important  true if this observation is important boolean NA lndcvr <-   read_tdr_csv("https://github.com/geomarker-io/hamilton_landcover/releases/download/v0.1.0" )  glimpse_tdr(lndcvr) |>   knitr::kable()

what: Reading and writing just the metadata for a tabular-data-resource
title: Reading and writing tabular data resources
text: can useful read download metadata associated tabular-data-resource object. read_tdr() function , returns list two items: (1) tabular-data-resource metadata list (2) file path URL data file, generated expressing path relative location tabular-data-resource.yaml file specified. return different csv_file tabular-data-resource specified using absolute file path: also works URL:
code: read_tdr("mydata") |>   str(2) #> List of 2 #>  $ tdr     :List of 7 #>   ..$ profile : chr "tabular-data-resource" #>   ..$ name    : chr "mydata" #>   ..$ path    : chr "mydata.csv" #>   ..$ version : chr "0.1.0" #>   ..$ title   : chr "My Data" #>   ..$ homepage: chr "https://geomarker.io/CODECtools" #>   ..$ schema  :List of 1 #>  $ csv_file: 'fs_path' chr "mydata/mydata.csv" read_tdr("~/code/CODECtools/tests/testthat/d") |>   str(2) read_tdr("https://github.com/geomarker-io/hamilton_landcover/releases/download/v0.1.0") |>   str(4) #> List of 2 #>  $ tdr     :List of 7 #>   ..$ profile : chr "tabular-data-resource" #>   ..$ name    : chr "hamilton_landcover" #>   ..$ path    : chr "hamilton_landcover.csv" #>   ..$ version : chr "0.1.0" #>   ..$ title   : chr "Hamilton County Landcover and Built Environment Characteristics" #>   ..$ homepage: chr "https://geomarker.io/hamilton_landcover" #>   ..$ schema  :List of 1 #>   .. ..$ fields:List of 5 #>   .. .. ..$ census_tract_id    :List of 3 #>   .. .. ..$ pct_green_2019     :List of 4 #>   .. .. ..$ pct_impervious_2019:List of 4 #>   .. .. ..$ pct_treecanopy_2016:List of 4 #>   .. .. ..$ evi_2018           :List of 4 #>  $ csv_file: chr "https://github.com/geomarker-io/hamilton_landcover/releases/download/v0.1.0/hamilton_landcover.csv"

text: check_codec_tdr_csv() use functions check file structure correct metadata can read using CODEC specifications CSV data can read accompanying metadata data contains census tract identifier column data contains year (year month) column(s) fields CSV data described metadata vice-versa See vignette("codec-specs") CODEC specifications.

title: codec_tdr — codec_tdr
text: Defines list names (descriptions) valid CODEC property, schema, fields descriptors

title: codec_tdr — codec_tdr
text: list named character vectors, one "property", "schema", "fields"

title: codec_tdr — codec_tdr
code: codec_tdr()$property #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                profile  #>                                                                                                                                              "profile of this descriptor (always set to `tabular-data-resource` here)"  #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                   name  #>                                                                                                                               "an identifier string composed of lower case alphanumeric characters, `_`, `-`, and `.`"  #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                   path  #> "location of data associated with resource as a [POSIX path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Path_%28computing%29#POSIX_pathname_definition) relative to the `tabular-data-resource.yaml` file or a fully qualified URL"  #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                version  #>                                                                                                            "semantic [version](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/patterns/#data-package-version) of the data resource"  #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                  title  #>                                                                                                                                                                                 "human-friendly title of the resource"  #>                                                                                                                                                                                                               homepage  #>                                                                                                                           "homepage on the web related to the data; ideally a code repository used to create the data"  #>                                                                                                                                                                                                            description  #>                                                                                                                                                                                  "additional notes about the resource"  #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                 schema  #>                                                                                                                                                                             "a list object containing items in schema"  codec_tdr()$schema #>                                                                              fields  #> "a list object as long as the number of fields each containing the items in fields"  #>                                                                          primaryKey  #>                        "a field or set of fields that uniquely identifies each row"  #>                                                                          foreignKey  #>                         "a field or set of fields that connect to a separate table"  codec_tdr()$fields #>                                                                                                                                                      name  #>                                                             "machine-friendly name of field/column; must be identical to name of column in data CSV file"  #>                                                                                                                                                     title  #>                                                                                                                     "human-friendly name of field/column"  #>                                                                                                                                               description  #>                                                                                                             "any additional notes about the field/column"  #>                                                                                                                                                      type  #>              "[Frictionless type](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/table-schema/#types-and-formats) of the field/column (e.g., string, number, boolean)"  #>                                                                                                                                               constraints  #> "[Frictionless constraints](https://specs.frictionlessdata.io/table-schema/#constraints), including `enum`, an array of possible values or factor levels"

title: Tidy eval helpers — tidyeval
text: page lists tidy eval tools reexported package rlang. learn using tidy eval scripts packages high level, see dplyr programming vignette ggplot2 packages vignette. Metaprogramming section Advanced R may also useful deeper dive. tidy eval operators {{, !!, !!! syntactic constructs specially interpreted tidy eval functions. mostly need {{, !! !!! advanced operators use simple cases. curly-curly operator {{ allows tunnel data-variables passed function arguments inside tidy eval functions. {{ designed individual arguments. pass multiple arguments contained dots, use ... normal way.   enquo() enquos() delay execution one several function arguments. former returns single expression, latter returns list expressions. defused, expressions longer evaluate . must injected back evaluation context !! (single expression) !!! (list expressions).   simple case, code equivalent usage {{ ... . Defusing enquo() enquos() needed complex cases, instance need inspect modify expressions way. .data pronoun object represents current slice data. variable name string, use .data pronoun subset variable [[.   Another tidy eval operator :=. makes possible use glue curly-curly syntax LHS =. technical reasons, R language support complex expressions left =, use := workaround.   Many tidy eval functions like dplyr::mutate() dplyr::summarise() give automatic name unnamed inputs. need create sort automatic names , use as_label(). instance, glue-tunnelling syntax can reproduced manually :   Expressions defused enquo() (tunnelled {{) need simple column names, can arbitrarily complex. as_label() handles cases gracefully. code assumes simple column name, use as_name() instead. safer throws error input name expected.
code: my_function <- function(data, var, ...) {   data %>%     group_by(...) %>%     summarise(mean = mean({{ var }})) } my_function <- function(data, var, ...) {   # Defuse   var <- enquo(var)   dots <- enquos(...)    # Inject   data %>%     group_by(!!!dots) %>%     summarise(mean = mean(!!var)) } my_var <- "disp" mtcars %>% summarise(mean = mean(.data[[my_var]])) my_function <- function(data, var, suffix = "foo") {   # Use `{{` to tunnel function arguments and the usual glue   # operator `{` to interpolate plain strings.   data %>%     summarise("{{ var }}_mean_{suffix}" := mean({{ var }})) } my_function <- function(data, var, suffix = "foo") {   var <- enquo(var)   prefix <- as_label(var)   data %>%     summarise("{prefix}_mean_{suffix}" := mean(!!var)) }
